10.reason: Missing space before %c; should be "%d %c" to skip whitespace; otherwise may read spaces as operators.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing space before %c; should be "%d %c" to skip whitespace; otherwise may read spaces as operators.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing space before %c; should be "%d %c" to skip whitespace. Otherwise whitespace is read as operator, leading to incorrect parsing.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing space in the format string "%d %c" to skip whitespace before reading the operator, which causes incorrect parsing when the input expression contains spaces.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing space in format string "%d %c" to skip whitespace before reading the operator, causing incorrect parsing when the input contains spaces.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing space in format string causes incorrect parsing of input with spaces; should be "%d %c" to skip whitespace]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [scanf format "%d%c" does not skip whitespace, so it may read space characters as operators instead of the actual operator or '='. This causes incorrect parsing of expressions with spaces.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Same issue: missing space before %c.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Same issue: missing space before %c causes whitespace to be read as operator, corrupting the parsing.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing space in the format string "%d %c" to skip whitespace before reading the operator, leading to logic errors when processing spaced inputs.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing space in format string "%d %c" to skip whitespace before reading the next operator, leading to incorrect character reading.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing space in format string causes incorrect parsing of input with spaces; should be "%d %c" to skip whitespace]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: "%d%c" reads the next character immediately after the number, which could be a space, leading to wrong operator detection and premature termination or miscalculation.]
